---
id: "DM_R044"
title: "Derivation Implementation Standard Rule"
type: "rule"
category: "data_management"
date_created: "2025-09-29"
date_modified: "2025-09-29"
author: "Claude"
status: "active"
version: "1.0"
weight: 44
implements:
  - "MP064": "ETL-Derivation Separation Principle"
  - "MP031": "Initialization First"
  - "MP033": "Deinitialization Final"
related_to:
  - "DM_R042": "DRV Sequential Numbering Rule"
  - "DEV_R032": "Script Structure Standard Rule"
  - "DM_R041": "ETL Directory Structure Rule"
  - "DM_R039": "Database Connection Pattern"
supersedes:
  - "Legacy derivation patterns without standardized structure"
---

# DM_R044: Derivation Implementation Standard Rule

## Rule Statement

**All Derivation (DRV) files MUST follow a standardized implementation structure with mandatory header format, five-part execution pattern, and comprehensive documentation blocks**: This ensures consistency, maintainability, and proper resource management across all derivation implementations.

## Standard Derivation Format

### Required Header Structure

Every derivation file MUST begin with this standardized header:

```r
#####
# DERIVATION: {NAME}
# VERSION: {X.Y}
# PLATFORM: {cbz|amz|eby|all}
# GROUP: D{nn}
# SEQUENCE: {seq}
# PURPOSE: {one-line description}
# CONSUMES: {ETL output tables}
# PRODUCES: {derived tables/views}
# PRINCIPLE: {relevant principles}
#####
#P{platform_number}_D{group}_{sequence}
```

**Header Components**:
- **DERIVATION**: Human-readable name (e.g., "Customer DNA Analysis")
- **VERSION**: Semantic version (e.g., "1.2")
- **PLATFORM**: Three-letter code or 'all' for cross-platform
- **GROUP**: Derivation group number (D01, D02, etc.)
- **SEQUENCE**: Execution sequence within group (03, 04, 05, etc.)
- **PURPOSE**: One-line description of what this derivation does
- **CONSUMES**: List of ETL output tables this derivation reads
- **PRODUCES**: List of tables/views this derivation creates
- **PRINCIPLE**: References to governing principles (MP064, DM_R042, etc.)
- **P-number**: Internal processing code matching filename (line 2)

### Documentation Block (Required)

Following the header, include roxygen2-style documentation:

```r
#' @title {Descriptive Title}
#' @description {Detailed description of derivation logic}
#' @param {parameter_name} {description} (if applicable)
#' @return {description of output}
#' @requires {required packages}
#' @input_tables {list of input tables from ETL}
#' @output_tables {list of output tables produced}
#' @business_rules {business logic documentation}
#' @platform {platform_id}
#' @author {original author}
#' @modified_by {modifier name}
#' @date {creation date}
#' @update_log {update history}
```

## Five-Part Execution Structure

All derivation files MUST implement exactly five sections:

### PART 1: INITIALIZE

```r
# ==============================================================================
# PART 1: INITIALIZE
# ==============================================================================
# Setup environment and connections

# 1.1: Environment Setup
autoinit()  # or equivalent initialization

# 1.2: Database Connections
# Track which connections this script creates
connection_created_processed <- FALSE
connection_created_app <- FALSE

# Connect to required databases
if (!exists("processed_data") || !inherits(processed_data, "DBIConnection")) {
  processed_data <- dbConnectDuckdb(db_path_list$processed_data, read_only = TRUE)
  connection_created_processed <- TRUE
  message("Connected to processed_data database")
}

if (!exists("app_data") || !inherits(app_data, "DBIConnection")) {
  app_data <- dbConnectDuckdb(db_path_list$app_data, read_only = FALSE)
  connection_created_app <- TRUE
  message("Connected to app_data database")
}

# 1.3: Load Dependencies
# Source any required utility functions
# Load configuration parameters

# 1.4: Initialize Tracking Variables
error_occurred <- FALSE
test_passed <- FALSE
rows_processed <- 0
start_time <- Sys.time()
```

### PART 2: MAIN

```r
# ==============================================================================
# PART 2: MAIN
# ==============================================================================
# Core derivation logic

tryCatch({
  # 2.1: Log Processing Start
  message(sprintf("Starting %s at %s", basename(commandArgs()[4]), start_time))

  # 2.2: Validate Input Tables
  required_tables <- c("df_sales_standardized", "df_customer_standardized")
  for (table in required_tables) {
    if (!dbExistsTable(processed_data, table)) {
      stop(sprintf("Required input table %s does not exist", table))
    }
  }

  # 2.3: Load ETL Output Data
  df_input <- tbl(processed_data, "df_sales_standardized") %>%
    collect()

  message(sprintf("Loaded %d rows from input table", nrow(df_input)))

  # 2.4: Apply Business Logic
  # This is where derivation-specific logic goes
  # Example: Customer aggregation, RFM calculation, etc.

  df_derived <- df_input %>%
    group_by(customer_id, platform_id) %>%
    summarize(
      total_revenue = sum(lineproduct_price, na.rm = TRUE),
      transaction_count = n(),
      first_purchase = min(payment_time, na.rm = TRUE),
      last_purchase = max(payment_time, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      days_since_last = as.numeric(Sys.Date() - as.Date(last_purchase)),
      customer_lifetime = as.numeric(as.Date(last_purchase) - as.Date(first_purchase))
    )

  rows_processed <- nrow(df_derived)

  # 2.5: Write Derived Output
  dbWriteTable(
    app_data,
    "df_customer_metrics",
    df_derived,
    append = FALSE,
    overwrite = TRUE
  )

  message(sprintf("Wrote %d rows to output table", rows_processed))

}, error = function(e) {
  message("ERROR in MAIN: ", e$message)
  error_occurred <- TRUE
})
```

### PART 3: TEST

```r
# ==============================================================================
# PART 3: TEST
# ==============================================================================
# Verify derivation results

if (!error_occurred) {
  tryCatch({
    # 3.1: Verify Output Table Exists
    if (!dbExistsTable(app_data, "df_customer_metrics")) {
      stop("Output table was not created")
    }

    # 3.2: Validate Output Structure
    output_check <- tbl(app_data, "df_customer_metrics") %>%
      head(5) %>%
      collect()

    required_columns <- c("customer_id", "platform_id", "total_revenue",
                         "transaction_count", "days_since_last")
    missing_columns <- setdiff(required_columns, colnames(output_check))

    if (length(missing_columns) > 0) {
      stop(sprintf("Missing required columns: %s",
                  paste(missing_columns, collapse = ", ")))
    }

    # 3.3: Validate Business Rules
    # Check for data quality issues
    invalid_records <- output_check %>%
      filter(total_revenue < 0 | transaction_count < 1)

    if (nrow(invalid_records) > 0) {
      warning(sprintf("Found %d records violating business rules",
                     nrow(invalid_records)))
    }

    # 3.4: Check Row Counts
    output_rows <- tbl(app_data, "df_customer_metrics") %>%
      summarize(n = n()) %>%
      pull(n)

    if (output_rows != rows_processed) {
      warning(sprintf("Row count mismatch: processed %d, wrote %d",
                     rows_processed, output_rows))
    }

    message("✅ All tests passed successfully")
    test_passed <- TRUE

  }, error = function(e) {
    message("ERROR in TEST: ", e$message)
    test_passed <- FALSE
  })
} else {
  message("Skipping tests due to error in MAIN")
  test_passed <- FALSE
}
```

### PART 4: SUMMARIZE

```r
# ==============================================================================
# PART 4: SUMMARIZE
# ==============================================================================
# Report metrics and prepare return values

# 4.1: Calculate Execution Metrics
end_time <- Sys.time()
execution_time <- difftime(end_time, start_time, units = "secs")

# 4.2: Generate Summary Report
summary_report <- list(
  script = basename(commandArgs()[4]),
  platform = "cbz",  # or extracted from filename
  group = "D01",
  sequence = "03",
  start_time = start_time,
  end_time = end_time,
  execution_time_secs = as.numeric(execution_time),
  rows_processed = rows_processed,
  status = ifelse(test_passed, "SUCCESS", "FAILED"),
  error_occurred = error_occurred
)

# 4.3: Log Summary
message("\n" %+% paste(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""))
message("DERIVATION SUMMARY")
message(paste(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""))
message(sprintf("Script: %s", summary_report$script))
message(sprintf("Status: %s", summary_report$status))
message(sprintf("Rows Processed: %d", summary_report$rows_processed))
message(sprintf("Execution Time: %.2f seconds", summary_report$execution_time_secs))
message(paste(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""))

# 4.4: Write Execution Log (optional)
if (exists("execution_log_path")) {
  log_entry <- data.frame(summary_report, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  write.csv(log_entry,
           file.path(execution_log_path,
                    sprintf("drv_log_%s.csv", format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S"))),
           row.names = FALSE)
}

# 4.5: Prepare Return Value
return_value <- summary_report
```

### PART 5: DEINITIALIZE

```r
# ==============================================================================
# PART 5: DEINITIALIZE
# ==============================================================================
# MANDATORY: Cleanup and resource deallocation ONLY

# 5.1: Close Database Connections (only those created by this script)
if (exists("connection_created_processed") && connection_created_processed) {
  if (exists("processed_data") && inherits(processed_data, "DBIConnection")) {
    dbDisconnect(processed_data)
    message("Disconnected from processed_data database")
  }
}

if (exists("connection_created_app") && connection_created_app) {
  if (exists("app_data") && inherits(app_data, "DBIConnection")) {
    dbDisconnect(app_data)
    message("Disconnected from app_data database")
  }
}

# 5.2: Return Final Value
if (exists("return_value")) {
  final_status <- return_value
} else {
  final_status <- list(status = "INCOMPLETE", error = "No return value generated")
}

# 5.3: Autodeinit (MUST be last statement)
autodeinit()

# Return the final status
final_status
```

## Error Handling Requirements

### Graceful Degradation

All derivations MUST handle errors gracefully:

```r
# Pattern for non-critical operations
tryCatch({
  # Optional enhancement
  df_enhanced <- add_optional_metrics(df_base)
}, error = function(e) {
  warning(sprintf("Could not add optional metrics: %s", e$message))
  df_enhanced <- df_base  # Continue with base data
})
```

### Critical Failures

For critical failures that prevent derivation completion:

```r
# Pattern for critical operations
tryCatch({
  # Critical operation
  df_required <- load_required_data()
}, error = function(e) {
  message(sprintf("CRITICAL ERROR: %s", e$message))
  error_occurred <- TRUE
  # Do not continue processing
})
```

## Input/Output Documentation

### Table Dependency Declaration

Each derivation MUST clearly document its data dependencies:

```yaml
# At top of file in comments or documentation block
INPUT_TABLES:
  processed_data:
    - df_sales_standardized     # From ETL01_2TR
    - df_customer_standardized  # From ETL02_2TR
    - df_product_standardized   # From ETL03_2TR

OUTPUT_TABLES:
  app_data:
    - df_customer_dna           # Customer DNA profiles
    - df_customer_segments      # Customer segmentation
    - df_customer_metrics       # Aggregated metrics
```

### Column Specifications

Document expected column structures:

```yaml
EXPECTED_COLUMNS:
  df_sales_standardized:
    required:
      - customer_id (INTEGER)
      - platform_id (TEXT)
      - lineproduct_price (NUMERIC)
      - payment_time (TIMESTAMP)
    optional:
      - product_line_id (INTEGER)
      - discount_amount (NUMERIC)
```

## Platform-Specific Considerations

### Platform ID Validation

```r
# Extract and validate platform from filename
get_platform_from_filename <- function() {
  script_name <- basename(commandArgs()[4])
  platform <- substr(script_name, 1, 3)

  valid_platforms <- c("cbz", "amz", "eby", "all")
  if (!platform %in% valid_platforms) {
    stop(sprintf("Invalid platform_id: %s", platform))
  }

  return(platform)
}
```

### Cross-Platform Derivations

For derivations that process multiple platforms:

```r
# Pattern for cross-platform processing
platforms <- c("cbz", "amz", "eby")

results_list <- lapply(platforms, function(platform) {
  tryCatch({
    process_platform_data(platform)
  }, error = function(e) {
    warning(sprintf("Failed to process platform %s: %s",
                   platform, e$message))
    return(NULL)
  })
})

# Combine non-null results
df_combined <- do.call(rbind, Filter(Negate(is.null), results_list))
```

## Logging Standards

### Message Formatting

Use consistent message formatting:

```r
# Progress messages
message(sprintf("[%s] Starting derivation: %s",
               format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),
               "Customer DNA Analysis"))

# Warning messages
warning(sprintf("DM_R044 WARNING: %s", "Missing optional field"))

# Error messages
message(sprintf("DM_R044 ERROR: %s", "Required table not found"))

# Success messages
message("✅ DM_R044: Derivation completed successfully")
```

### Execution Tracking

Track key metrics throughout execution:

```r
# Initialize metrics
metrics <- list(
  start_time = Sys.time(),
  rows_read = 0,
  rows_processed = 0,
  rows_written = 0,
  warnings = 0,
  errors = 0
)

# Update during processing
metrics$rows_read <- nrow(df_input)
metrics$rows_processed <- metrics$rows_processed + batch_size
```

## Validation Functions

### Pre-Derivation Validation

```r
validate_derivation_prerequisites <- function() {
  # Check required databases exist
  required_dbs <- c("processed_data", "app_data")
  for (db in required_dbs) {
    if (!exists(db) || !inherits(get(db), "DBIConnection")) {
      stop(sprintf("Required database connection missing: %s", db))
    }
  }

  # Check required tables exist
  if (!dbExistsTable(processed_data, "df_sales_standardized")) {
    stop("Required ETL output table missing: df_sales_standardized")
  }

  # Check table has data
  row_count <- tbl(processed_data, "df_sales_standardized") %>%
    summarize(n = n()) %>%
    pull(n)

  if (row_count == 0) {
    warning("Input table is empty, derivation may produce no results")
  }

  return(TRUE)
}
```

### Post-Derivation Validation

```r
validate_derivation_output <- function(table_name, expected_rows = NULL) {
  # Check table exists
  if (!dbExistsTable(app_data, table_name)) {
    stop(sprintf("Output table not created: %s", table_name))
  }

  # Check row count if expected value provided
  if (!is.null(expected_rows)) {
    actual_rows <- tbl(app_data, table_name) %>%
      summarize(n = n()) %>%
      pull(n)

    if (actual_rows != expected_rows) {
      warning(sprintf("Row count mismatch: expected %d, got %d",
                     expected_rows, actual_rows))
    }
  }

  # Check for required columns
  sample_data <- tbl(app_data, table_name) %>%
    head(1) %>%
    collect()

  if (nrow(sample_data) == 0) {
    warning(sprintf("Output table %s is empty", table_name))
  }

  return(TRUE)
}
```

## Migration from Legacy Patterns

### Identify Non-Compliant Files

```r
# Find derivation files not following standard
find_non_compliant_derivations <- function(drv_dir) {
  all_files <- list.files(drv_dir, pattern = "\\.R$",
                         recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)

  non_compliant <- c()

  for (file in all_files) {
    lines <- readLines(file, n = 20)

    # Check for standard header
    if (!any(grepl("^# DERIVATION:", lines))) {
      non_compliant <- c(non_compliant, file)
      next
    }

    # Check for five-part structure
    required_parts <- c("PART 1: INITIALIZE", "PART 2: MAIN",
                       "PART 3: TEST", "PART 4: SUMMARIZE",
                       "PART 5: DEINITIALIZE")

    file_content <- readLines(file)
    missing_parts <- required_parts[!sapply(required_parts, function(part) {
      any(grepl(part, file_content, fixed = TRUE))
    })]

    if (length(missing_parts) > 0) {
      non_compliant <- c(non_compliant, file)
    }
  }

  return(non_compliant)
}
```

## Implementation Checklist

- [ ] Header contains all required fields (DERIVATION, VERSION, etc.)
- [ ] P-number on line 2 matches filename pattern
- [ ] Documentation block with roxygen2-style annotations
- [ ] Five-part structure clearly marked with separators
- [ ] INITIALIZE section sets up environment and connections
- [ ] MAIN section wrapped in tryCatch for error handling
- [ ] TEST section validates output and business rules
- [ ] SUMMARIZE section generates metrics and reports
- [ ] DEINITIALIZE section performs cleanup with autodeinit() last
- [ ] Input/output tables clearly documented
- [ ] Platform ID validation implemented
- [ ] Consistent logging and message formatting
- [ ] Return value properly structured

## Benefits

### Consistency
- All derivations follow same structure
- Easy to understand and maintain
- Predictable execution flow

### Maintainability
- Clear documentation requirements
- Standardized error handling
- Comprehensive logging

### Reliability
- Proper resource management
- Guaranteed cleanup
- Validated inputs/outputs

### Traceability
- Execution metrics tracked
- Clear data lineage
- Audit trail maintained

## Relationship to Other Rules

### Implements
- **MP064**: Maintains ETL-Derivation separation
- **MP031**: Ensures initialization happens first
- **MP033**: Guarantees deinitialization is final

### Complements
- **DM_R042**: Works with sequential numbering system
- **DEV_R032**: Extends five-part structure standard
- **DM_R041**: Follows directory structure requirements

### Supersedes
- Legacy ad-hoc derivation patterns
- Unstructured derivation implementations
- Three-part or four-part structures

## Conclusion

The Derivation Implementation Standard (DM_R044) establishes a comprehensive framework for all derivation files, ensuring consistency, maintainability, and reliability across the data processing pipeline. This standard provides clear guidelines for structure, documentation, error handling, and validation, creating a robust foundation for business logic implementation.
